Me: Yeah, we go straight ahead.

Me: Yeah, no, it's. It's great to jump on, you know, for context, for us. James has been such an incredible advisor, has been sort of providing us with. With such good guidance over the last two years, really. He was the first person we brought on. And so, you know, it's. It's been great to have him as a resource and. And to give you context for the company. We started off as a career coaching tool, and that's still a pretty significant part of what we do. So Logan and I are both career coaches. You know, spent thousands of hours helping people sort of build better careers. And along the way, sort of what we realized is that the big gap for most folks is how they put themselves forward, right? You could be the most qualified person in the world. But if you don't align yourself effectively, if you don't highlight the relevant things in your background, you kind of are stuck. And so that was the first problem that we decided to solve. Built a great coaching tool to help people do that. Realized along the way that in this process, we'd actually learned, we'd actually built a really great way to get to know people. Right? Get to know them on a deeper level, not just, you know, what achievement they had or what skill they've honed. But actually the traceability around how did they hone it right? Letting you compare cross context for folks. And when we sort of have that realization, we said, great. This way, we can give the tool for free the candidates. It gives everyone a chance to sort of build out their best possible story. And we can use that data to match them to employers that are looking for jobs. And that's effectively brings us to the present moment. We've built a decentralized talent network that draws from the best professional communities online. Sort of bringing them all onto platform and giving you the ability to quick scan against the folks that have been onboarded onto careers, found identify a great candidate. Sort of pause there, answer any questions, and then also allow Logan to fill in any gaps that I may have left behind.

What it effectively does is it decomposes your job description. Now, what it also does is it gives the candidates enough of a head start to say, hey, these are gaps in your story. Maybe you can fill them, maybe you can. If you can fill them. Here's a certain amount of time for you to get that done. And we'll update the employer and say, hey, actually, this candidate is more fitted, right? Because that's the other sort of discrepancy that we noticed is that it's not just that some people just lacks something. In which case, you know, we have systems to sort of tell, hey, is this person being legitimate? But for most folks, the problem is that they probably have something they just haven't been able to highlight it. Right. Or they haven't been able to pull it across. So we actually address both. And that's how because we embed with employers or with candidates over a long period of time. It's almost as though they spend a month filling out of workday profile. Right. But you get to scan that in a second. It's sort of the best of both barrels, where they can put in a ton of effort, but your actual amount red or stuff to go through doesn't increase.
